title: rothmannia coriacea
intro: Rothmannia coriacea, also known as the wild coffee tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its distinctive red or yellow flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rothmannia coriacea include reducing fever, treating coughs and colds, and providing relief from pain and inflammation. The herbal preparations of Rothmannia coriacea typically involve brewing the leaves or fruits as a tea, or using the plant's extracts in capsule or tincture form.
